title: All-On-4 Implants Made Easy
article: The All-on-4 dental implant procedure is widely favored for its reliability and durability. Numerous individuals prefer this method. Nonetheless, it's common to experience some apprehension before undergoing the treatment. To alleviate any anxiety, we will outline each phase of the procedure, which can help you feel more comfortable and reassured.

title: All-On-4 Implants Made Easy
article:
Step 1: Your First Consultation

First, you will have a consultation. Your dental health and medical history are reviewed. Any issues are addressed. Once you understand the procedure and are deemed suitable, the process moves forward.

title: All-On-4 Implants Made Easy
article:
Step 2: Preparing For Your Implants

The dentist measures your mouth for accurate implant placement. This ensures a perfect fit and stability. Necessary pretreatments like tooth extractions or bone grafting are done. This prepares your mouth for the implants.

title: All-On-4 Implants Made Easy
article:
Step 3: Placing Your Implants

You will receive anesthesia for comfort. The dentist places four titanium screws in your jaw. These screws act as a foundation for the new teeth. It takes 3-6 months for the screws to bond with the jawbone. You will wear temporary dentures during this time.

title: All-On-4 Implants Made Easy
article:
Step 4: Attaching The Abutments

Once the implants fuse with your jawbone, abutments are attached. These hold the artificial teeth securely. The dentist makes molds of your mouth. These molds are sent to a dental lab to create your custom teeth. You can choose removable or permanent teeth.

title: All-On-4 Implants Made Easy
article:
Step 5: Fitting Your New Teeth

The dental lab finishes your prosthetic. It is attached to the abutments. The dentist adjusts it for a perfect fit. The All-on-4 procedure is complete once this step is done.
